subject: Re: MCore Error due to: System.IO.DirectoryNotFoundException: Could not find a part of the path '...m/refinement_temp/c_elegans/worker0'.
body:
I’m surprised the diameter made such a huge difference but glad you found a fix and your results are so good, awesome job!!
Cheers,
Alister
Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
On Nov 23, 2024, at 06:38, Euan William Pyle <euan.pyle@embl.de> wrote:
﻿Fixed the issue now: I had set the create_species particle diameter to 360 A for a ribosome, reducing this to 300 A has yielded a 6 A structure after the first M iteration...happy!
WarpAndM4Eva
Euan
On Friday, 22 November 2024 at 20:23:29 UTC+1 alist...@gmail.com wrote:
Particle coordinates should end up in angstroms, defined relative to the voxel at [0, 0, 0] in the tomogram
On Fri, 22 Nov 2024 at 11:12, Euan William Pyle <
euan...@embl.de
> wrote:
Sadly, MCore now gives a 900 Å structure which looks like a grid. Should the particles.star file outputed by create_species contain particle coordinates in unbinned pixels? If not then I totally misidentified the issue.
THanks
Euan
On Friday, 22 November 2024 at 19:16:42 UTC+1 Euan William Pyle wrote:
Thanks! So I played with those just now (entering --angpix_coords/shifts 4.78 which is the correct pixel size) and the resulting particles.star from create_species looks the same as before (incorrect). I noticed the resulting .star from create_species contained coordinates that were multiplied by 4.78. So, in the end, what worked for me was:
relion_star_downgrade -s [run_data.star result from Refinement at bin2] #to add shifts to the coordinates
and when running create_species, instead of specifying the angpix of the subtomograms refined in angpix_coord/shifts, specifying the binning of the particles instead:
--angpix_resample 
2.39
 
\
--angpix_coords 
2
 
\
--angpix_shifts 
2
This gave the correct result in the particles.star generated by create_species in unbinned pixel coordinates.
I'm not sure why this works, I suspect it has to do with the fact I'm using 3D subtomograms as opposed to 2D stacks, and that my coordinates are in px and not centred coordinates or angstrom (or something like that).
I'm just waiting now for MCore to run, if it still gives me a bad resolution, I'll message again, but I'd be surprised, I think it's fixed now ;) (hopefully I haven't cursed it by saying that)
Thanks,
Euan
On Friday, 22 November 2024 at 18:26:31 UTC+1 
alist...@gmail.com
 wrote:
Hi Euan,
Looks like you need to supply `--angpix_coords` and potentially `--angpix_shifts` to make sure you get the right coordinates :-)
https://warpem.github.io/warp/reference/mtools/api/mtools/#create_species
Cheers,
Alister
On Friday, 22 November 2024 at 09:07:10 UTC-8 
euan...@embl.de
 wrote:
So I think I have found the culprit. I think M is doing something strange to the star files. When I give it a .star file (coordinates binned by 2, so max X/Y dimensions = approx. 2000) that looks like this: 
=========================================================
# version 30001
data_optics
loop_ 
_rlnOpticsGroup #1 
_rlnOpticsGroupName #2 
_rlnSphericalAberration #3 
_rlnVoltage #4 
_rlnImagePixelSize #5 
_rlnImageSize #6 
_rlnImageDimensionality #7 
           1 opticsGroup1     2.700000   300.000000     4.780000           96            3 
 
# version 30001
data_particles
loop_ 
_rlnCoordinateX #1 
_rlnCoordinateY #2 
_rlnCoordinateZ #3 
...
...
368.060000  1368.060000   744.060000    -28.93335    71.432991   113.227920 TS_0071.tomostar     8.400000 ../warp_tiltseries/group_1/subtomo/TS_0071/TS_0071_0000000_4.78A.mrc ../warp_tiltseries/group_1/subtomo/TS_0071/TS_0071_0000000_ctf_4.78A.mrc     4.780000            1            1     -4.22610     5.333901     5.333901            1     1.000000            1 2.984956e+06     0.234658           10 
 1071.432000    85.001000   404.060000   174.982458    68.345719    -10.55141 TS_0065.tomostar     7.200000 ../warp_tiltseries/group_1/subtomo/TS_0065/TS_0065_0000000_4.78A.mrc ../warp_tiltseries/group_1/subtomo/TS_0065/TS_0065_0000000_ctf_4.78A.mrc     4.780000            1            1     0.553901     -0.84425     0.553901            1     1.000000            1 2.978322e+06     0.131800            8 
  932.688000  1938.060000   444.688000    63.409574    43.800054    34.390241 TS_0065.tomostar     7.200000 ../warp_tiltseries/group_1/subtomo/TS_0065/TS_0065_0000001_4.78A.mrc ../warp_tiltseries/group_1/subtomo/TS_0065/TS_0065_0000001_ctf_4.78A.mrc     4.780000            1            1     -0.84425     -0.84425     5.333901            1     1.000000            2 2.980775e+06     0.073959           14 
=========================================================
and use the following create species command (rescaling to bin1 which is 2.39 Å):
MTools
 
create_species
 
\
--population 
m/c_elegans.population
 
\
--name 
ribosome_initial
 
\
--diameter 
360
 
\
--temporal_samples 
1
 
\
--half1  
relion_ribosomes/Refine3D/job020/run_half1_class001_unfil.mrc
 
\
--half2  
relion_ribosomes/Refine3D/job020/run_half2_class001_unfil.mrc
 
\
--mask 
relion_ribosomes/MaskCreate/job040/mask.mrc
 
\
--particles_relion 
relion_ribosomes/Refine3D/job020/run_data.star
 
\
--angpix_resample 
2.39
 
then the [Species_Name]_particle.star in m/species/[species_name]/[species_name]_particles.star has totally wrong coordinates which look like they've been multplied a lot:
=========================================================
data_
loop_
_wrpCoordinateX1 #1
_wrpCoordinateY1 #2
_wrpCoordinateZ1 #3
_wrpAngleRot1 #4
_wrpAngleTilt1 #5
_wrpAnglePsi1 #6
_wrpRandomSubset #7
_wrpSourceName #8
_wrpSourceHash #9
  1763.55298  6533.99316  3551.27295   -28.93335   71.43299   113.22792  1  TS_0071.tomostar  ZTx8rfiNPvMw0Xze-kry6nP9KYY
  5120.89160   407.14905  1930.85291   174.98245   68.34572   -10.55141  1  TS_0065.tomostar  NOBDUlh2lIXbCXxbftrH6l2w5UQ
  4459.09326  9264.77246  2120.27466    63.40957   43.80005    34.39024  2  TS_0065.tomostar  NOBDUlh2lIXbCXxbftrH6l2w5UQ
=========================================================
Will do some more digging and will post a reply when I find a fix.
Thanks
Euan
On Friday, 22 November 2024 at 12:31:32 UTC+1 Euan William Pyle wrote:
Awesome, thanks, that has solved the bug! 
One issue that is persisting however is that after running MCore as pasted below, my 11 Å structure becomes a 34 Å one, and the resulting denoised species looks like junk. The filtered species looks more like a ribosome, but very noisy, and the features that I used to see outside the map (i.e. neighbouring ribosomes) have disappeared which is suggesting to me that perhaps M is not reading the particles in the correct place and is fitting noise. I've tried with both the run_data.star and a relion_star_downgrade-d (by Dynamo2M) star when creating species (see below) and correctly specified angpix_coords, again for certainty.  I've also pasted the original .xml files back into warp_tiltseries etc., and re-extracted particles at bin1, and run relion_reconstruct, and my ribosome appears looking very nice, so I'm a bit at a loss as to what's happening.
Thanks again,
Euan
MCore
 
\
--population 
m/c_elegans.population
 
\
--refine_imagewarp 
4x4
 
\
--refine_particles 
\
--perdevice_refine 
4
MTools
 
create_species
 
\
--population 
m/c_elegans.population
 
\
--name 
job032_ribosome
 
\
--diameter 
360
 
\
--temporal_samples 
1
 
\
--half1 
relion_ribosomes/Reconstruct/job032_half1_class001_unfil.mrc
 
\
--half2 
relion_ribosomes/Reconstruct/job032_half2_class001_unfil.mrc
 
\
--mask 
relion_ribosomes/MaskCreate/job038/mask.mrc
 
\
--particles_relion 
./job032_particles.star
 
\
--lowpass 
17
 
\
--angpix_resample 
2.39
 
\
--angpix_coords 
4.78
 
\
--angpix_shifts 
4.78
On Thursday, 21 November 2024 at 23:58:41 UTC+1 
alist...@gmail.com
 wrote:
2.0.0dev30 available once this finishes... 
https://github.com/warpem/warp/actions/runs/11963052857
On Thu, 21 Nov 2024 at 14:50, Alister Burt <
alist...@gmail.com
> wrote:
Hi Euan,
Thanks for the kind words and the report, I found the bug and will push up a fix now - the only thing that is supposed to happen after the line where your refinement crashed is saving the results. Given that your refinement has run without actually refining anything (--iter 0) you can safely move on to actual refinements without worrying :-)
I'd appreciate if you could report back whether you see the same issue with normal refinements...
Cheers,
Alister
On Thu, 21 Nov 2024 at 14:33, Euan William Pyle <
euan...@embl.de
> wrote:
Hey gang, 
first thing - WARPTools is awesome and helping me a lot.
I'm trying out M now, and have come across some strange behaviour.
On a previous dataset, it worked absolutely fine, no issues.
However, on my current dataset I'm having issues running: 
MCore \
--population m/c_elegans.population \
--iter 0
See the commands and the error message below.
A few things that might be noteworthy:
1. I'm using dev version 29.
2. I'm extracting subtomos in 3D and refining in rln4 (this worked fine on other dataset)
3. I have 5 data sources which are arranged in directories like so: 
    ./warp_tiltseries/group_1/
    ./warp_tiltseries/group_2/
    ./warp_tiltseries/group_3/
    etc.
As per the error, the m/refinement_temp/c_elegans is already empty.
Strangely too, the resolution of the map was 10 A or so prior to M
and M reports a resolution of 38.24 here after refining. 
Any ideas on what's going on? I feel a bit stooopid as I can't quite get what's going wrong.
Thanks!
Euan
P.S. I tried to revert back to the pre-M state by copying the original .xml files
from versions and placing them back in the warp_tiltseries directory
and then deleting the .source files and the M directory. Hopefully this didn't mess things up.
I re-extracted particles and ran relion_reconstruct as a sanity check and they look fine 
====================================
COMMANDS
MTools create_population \
--directory m \
--name c_elegans
MTools create_source \
--name c_elegans \
--population m/c_elegans.population \
--processing_settings group_5_tiltseries.settings
#Repeat for groups 1 to 4.
MTools create_species \
--population m/c_elegans.population \
--name ribosome \
--diameter 360 \
--temporal_samples 1 \
--half1 relion_ribosomes/Refine3D/job020/run_half1_class001_unfil.mrc \
--half2 relion_ribosomes/Refine3D/job020/run_half2_class001_unfil.mrc \
--mask relion_ribosomes/MaskCreate/job039/mask.mrc \
--particles_relion relion_ribosomes/Refine3D/job020/run_data.star \
--lowpass 11 \
--angpix_resample 2.39
MCore \
--population m/c_elegans.population \
--iter 0
====================================
ERROR

...
...
